slides.title: Strict by Default 👮
slides.content:
In JavaScript, if a function asks for 3 arguments but you only pass 1, it runs anyway (the missing ones become `undefined`).

TypeScript is stricter. If it asks for 3 parameters, you MUST provide 3 arguments, or it throws an error. But what if a parameter is truly optional?

slides.title: Optional Parameters ❓
slides.content: You can make a parameter optional by placing a question mark `?` right after its name and before the colon.

quizData.question: What symbol is used to mark a parameter as optional?
quizData.explanation: Example: `lastName?: string` makes `lastName` an optional argument.

slides.title: The Rule of Position 📏
slides.content:
Optional parameters must *always* come after all required parameters.

`function test(optional?: string, required: string)` is invalid! You can't ask for a required value after an optional one.

slides.title: Default Parameters 🛡️
slides.content: Instead of marking it optional, you can provide a default value. If the caller doesn't provide it, your default is used automatically.
